recommendations.title: Bang Bang You're Dead
recommendations.overview: A troubled and bullied high school student fights against judgement from his community after threatening to bomb the football team.

recommendations.title: Killers of the Flower Moon
recommendations.overview: When oil is discovered in 1920s Oklahoma under Osage Nation land, the Osage people are murdered one by one—until the FBI steps in to unravel the mystery.

recommendations.title: National Lampoon Presents Dorm Daze
recommendations.overview: When a foreign-exchange student and a hooker - both named Dominique - show up on a college campus, the unsuspecting student body is in for the education of a lifetime!

recommendations.title: Freedom Writers
recommendations.overview: A young teacher inspires her class of at-risk students to learn tolerance, apply themselves, and pursue education beyond high school.

recommendations.title: Little Fish
recommendations.overview: Set in the Little Saigon district outside of Sydney, a woman trying to escape her past becomes embroiled in a drug deal.

recommendations.title: Call Jane
recommendations.overview: A married woman with an unwanted pregnancy lives in a time in America where she can't get a legal abortion and works with a group of suburban women to find help.

recommendations.title: Argentina 1985
recommendations.overview: In the 1980s, a team of lawyers takes on the heads of Argentina's bloody military dictatorship in a battle against odds and a race against time.

recommendations.title: I'm Not Ashamed
recommendations.overview: Based on the journal entries of Rachel Joy Scott, the first student killed in the Columbine High School shooting in 1999.

recommendations.title: JFK
recommendations.overview: Follows the investigation into the assassination of President John F. Kennedy led by New Orleans district attorney Jim Garrison.

recommendations.title: Red Dawn
recommendations.overview: On October 2, 1968, a student uprising descends into violence after the Mexican government begins to use lethal force against the protesters.
